docs.title: OneSurvey Help Center
docs.text:
OneSurvey helps teams plan site work, document field conditions, and track execution in one connected workflow.

Use this Help Center to get started quickly and keep projects moving from setup to completion.

Workspace overview across surveys, sites, and field operations.

docs.title: Start with These Actions
docs.text:
Create a Site Upload a Floor Plan Design & Document

For photo documentation during field work, use Link Photos.

docs.title: What OneSurvey Helps You Do
docs.text:
• Organize site work: create and manage sites, teams, and project context.
  • Capture and document field conditions: build surveys, place elements, and attach evidence.
  • Track execution: manage assignments, tickets, and reporting status in one place.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Set up access and create a site.
  2. Build or update the survey and document field conditions.
  3. Track assignments and tickets, then review progress through reports.

docs.title: Browse by Area

docs.title: Access Note
docs.text: What you see in OneSurvey depends on your role and seat type. See Access and Roles for details.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Sign In and Invitations
  • Users and Roles
  • Sites Overview
  • Surveys Overview
  • FAQ

docs.title: Images
docs.text:
Place your site logo / favicon at:

  • docs/assets/images/onesurvey-icon.png

MkDocs Material uses this file for both the header logo and favicon via the logo and favicon settings in mkdocs.yml.

Recommended sizes - Logo in header: 48–128 px square (PNG or SVG) - Favicon: 32×32 px and/or 48×48 px PNG also works well

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OneSurvey access is invitation-based. This page explains sign in, first-time setup, and what to do if you manage user invitations.

First-time account setup after accepting an invitation.

docs.title: Sign In
docs.text:
1. Open the OneSurvey login page.
  2. Enter your email and password.
  3. Select Sign In.

Notes: - Use the eye icon to show or hide your password. - If your organization enables Google sign-in, use Continue with Google.

If you cannot sign in, use Password Reset.

docs.title: If You Manage Users: Send an Invitation
docs.text:
Available to: Manager, Owner

  1. Open Users.
  2. Select Add User.
  3. Enter the person's email.
  4. Choose Seat Type and Org Role.
  5. Select Send Invite.

The person appears in the Users table with status Invited.

docs.title: Invitation Rules
docs.text:
• Manager and Owner roles require a Full seat.
  • Only an existing Owner can assign the Owner role.
  • If a seat limit is reached, the invite modal shows a capacity warning.
  • If the email already belongs to an existing OneSurvey account, the user receives an organization join email.

docs.title: Pending Invites
docs.text: From row actions on an invited user: - Resend invite: sends a new invite email. - Revoke invite: cancels the pending invitation.

docs.title: First-Time User Setup
docs.text:
1. Open the invitation email.
  2. Select Accept Invitation.
  3. Complete the required profile fields.
  4. Set and confirm password.
  5. Select Complete Setup.

After setup, you are signed in and sent to the Sites page.

Invitation email used to start setup.

docs.title: Joining Another Organization
docs.text:
If you already have a OneSurvey account and get invited to another organization:

  1. Open the invitation email.
  2. Select the organization link.
  3. Sign in with your existing account.
  4. Switch organizations with the organization selector if needed.

docs.title: Password Reset
docs.text:
1. On the login page, select Forgot Password.
  2. Enter your email.
  3. Open the reset email and use the link.
  4. Enter and confirm your new password.
  5. Sign in.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Problem What to do Invite email not received Check spam or junk. Ask your account manager or owner to resend the invite. Invite link expired Ask for a new invitation email. Invite link invalid The invite may have been revoked. Ask for a new invite. Cannot assign Owner role Only an existing Owner can assign Owner. Invite blocked by seat limit Ask an owner or manager to free seats or increase capacity. Signed in but missing expected sites Switch to the correct organization using the organization selector.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Users and Roles
  • Sites Overview
  • FAQ

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
OneSnap is a fast photo workflow for field capture, organization, and review.

OneSnap gallery for high-volume field photo capture.

docs.title: Session Tabs
docs.text:
• Gallery
  • Albums
  • Map
  • Reports
  • Archive
  • Floor Plan Builder (coming soon)

docs.title: What Teams Do in OneSnap
docs.text:
• Capture and review photos in Gallery.
  • Group photos in Albums.
  • Review location context in Map.
  • Generate share-ready outputs in Reports.
  • Move old content to Archive.

Map context with geotag support for captured photos.

docs.title: Sharing and Access
docs.text:
• Session access can be managed per user inside OneSnap.
  • Available actions vary by your role/seat and session access.

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: Floor Plan Builder currently shows as a maintenance/coming-soon area.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Mobile keeps work tracking close to field activity so updates happen in real time.

docs.title: Mobile Assignment Actions
docs.text:
• Review current work and due dates.
  • Update status and priority.
  • Add comments and attachments.
  • Jump to linked site and survey context.

docs.title: Mobile Ticket Actions
docs.text:
• Review active issues.
  • Update status, priority, and category.
  • Add comments and attachments.
  • Track resolution progress.

docs.title: Assignment Statuses
docs.text:
• To Do
  • In Progress
  • Blocked
  • Completed
  • Verified

docs.title: Ticket Statuses
docs.text:
• Open
  • Pending Vendor
  • In Progress
  • Waiting on Parts
  • Scheduled
  • Completed
  • Closed

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Assignments
  • Tickets
  • Mobile Overview

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: OneSurvey supports desktop, tablet, and phone. Each device is optimized for a different work style.

docs.title: Best Fit by Device
docs.text: Device Best for Desktop Full planning, site management, and organization administration. Tablet High-capability field execution and survey updates. Phone Fast updates, capture, and review while moving on site.

docs.title: Typical Guidance
docs.text:
• Use desktop for heavy planning and admin workflows.
  • Use tablet/phone for fast field updates, photos, assignments, and tickets.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Mobile Overview
  • Mobile Sites
  • Mobile Surveys

docs.title: Before You Arrive
docs.text:
• Confirm site access and assignment ownership.
  • Confirm your device is charged.
  • Confirm expected connectivity at site.

docs.title: While On Site
docs.text:
• Update statuses as work happens.
  • Attach photos directly to the right element.
  • Keep comments short and specific.
  • Use assignments and tickets to keep ownership clear.

docs.title: Before You Leave
docs.text:
• Check for incomplete assignments.
  • Confirm required photos were uploaded.
  • Confirm any active issues are logged as tickets.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Mobile Surveys
  • Assignments and Tickets

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Mobile OneSnap supports fast photo capture and review in the field.

docs.title: Common Actions
docs.text:

  • Capture photos.
  • Organize photos into albums.
  • Review photo locations in map view.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Mobile Sites gives fast access to assigned site work while on site.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Open your site list.
  • Open site details.
  • Jump into surveys, assignments, and tickets based on access.

docs.title: Typical Mobile Flow
docs.text:
1. Open the site.
  2. Open the target survey.
  3. Update element details and upload photos.
  4. Update related assignments and tickets.

docs.title: Settings Areas

• Profile.
  • Password.
  • Display.
  • Notifications.

docs.title: Notification Controls
docs.text:
Users can control email and in-app notifications for: - Assignments. - Comments and mentions. - Ticket updates. - Site/access updates.

Users can also set assignment due-date reminder timing.

  • Open an element detail view.

  • Upload photos.

  • Open related assignment or ticket details.

docs.text: Organization and access controls define who can work in OneSurvey and what each user can do.

docs.title: Access Basics
docs.text:
• Role controls authority: Owner, Manager, Member.
  • Seat controls capability: Full, Field, Viewer.
  • Visible menus and actions vary by role and seat.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Available to: Owner

Audit Logs gives owners a searchable history of key changes across sites, users, assignments, and tickets.

docs.title: Common Filters
docs.text:
• Search text
  • Site
  • User
  • Record type
  • Event type
  • Source
  • Date range

docs.title: What You Can Review
docs.text:
• Who made the change
  • What record changed
  • When it happened
  • Before and after field values

docs.title: Common Use Cases
docs.text:
• Confirm who changed a user's role or seat.
  • Review assignment or ticket status changes.
  • Validate site updates during handoff or QA.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Users and Roles
  • Assignments

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Available to: Manager, Owner

Clients are customer records tied to sites, OneSnap sessions, and ticket service expectations.

docs.title: Client Detail Tabs
docs.text:
• Basic Info: client profile and contact details.
  • Sites: linked sites for that client.
  • One Snaps: linked OneSnap sessions.
  • SLA: ticket service-level settings.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create or open a client record.
  2. Confirm contact and profile details.
  3. Link sites to the client.
  4. Review OneSnap activity and SLA expectations.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Sites Overview
  • Tickets
  • Users and Roles

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Available to: Manager, Owner

Use the Users page to invite people, set access, and track seat usage.

Users page for invitations, role assignment, and seat tracking.

docs.title: Organization Role
docs.text: Role Typical responsibility Owner Full organization authority, including assigning Owner access. Manager Manages users and operations across the organization. Member Works in sites based on seat type and project access.

docs.title: Seat Type
docs.text:
Seat Typical usage Full Full planning and editing workflows. Field Field execution workflows and updates. Viewer View and comment access with limited editing.

Manager and Owner roles require a Full seat.

docs.title: Invite a User
docs.text:
1. Open Users.
  2. Select Add User.
  3. Enter email.
  4. Choose seat type and role.
  5. Select Send Invite.

docs.title: Invitation Status
docs.text:
• New invitees show as Invited until setup is complete.
  • Use row actions to Resend invite or Revoke invite.
  • Existing OneSurvey users receive a join email for your organization.

docs.title: Update Access
docs.text:
1. Open row actions and select Edit.
  2. Update role and seat.
  3. Save.

Important behavior: - Only existing owners can assign Owner access. - The last owner in an organization cannot be demoted. - Access updates apply immediately.

docs.title: Remove a User
docs.text:
Use row actions and select Delete.

What happens: - If the person belongs only to your organization, their user record is removed. - If they belong to multiple organizations, they are removed from your organization only.

docs.title: Seat Usage
docs.text: The page shows Full, Field, and Viewer usage against seat limits so you can manage capacity before inviting more users.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Organization Settings
  • Audit Logs
  • Sign In and Invitations

docs.title: Sites Overview
docs.text:
Sites are the central workspace for planning, field execution, media capture, and final handoff.

Sites list with stage, team, and quick actions.

docs.title: Most Used
docs.text: Create a Site Site Detail Gallery Assignments Tickets Reports

Planning and Setup

  • Create a Site

Capture evidence, store files, and generate reports for delivery.

  • Assignments

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Surveys Overview
  • Mobile Overview

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Assignments track owned work items from creation through completion. They are used to assign specific tasks to team members, set ownership and priority, monitor progress, and keep execution work clearly accountable. Assignments can be linked to a specific survey or element so the assignee has direct context for where the work applies.

docs.title: Where Assignments Appear
docs.text:
• Site Detail → Assignments tab — All assignments for that specific site.
  • My Assignments (main left navigation) — A cross-site view of assignments relevant to you.

In the My Assignments view, toggle between:

  • My Assignments — Work currently assigned to you.
  • Assignments I've Sent — Work you have assigned to others.

docs.title: The Assignments Table
docs.text:
The Assignments tab displays a table with the following columns:

Column Description (Checkbox) Click the circular checkbox to mark the assignment complete. Title The assignment title. Shows a survey indicator if linked to a survey. Status Current status badge. Assignee The team member responsible. Priority Priority badge. Due Date The due date, if one was set. Survey The linked survey name, or a dash if not linked. Element The linked element, or a dash if not linked.

Click any row to open that assignment's detail and edit panel.

docs.title: Statuses
docs.text: Status Meaning To Do Created but not yet started. In Progress Actively being worked on. Blocked Work cannot proceed — something is preventing progress. Done Work is complete.

docs.title: Priorities
docs.text: Priority When to use it High Urgent work that needs immediate attention. Medium Standard priority for most tasks. Low Non-urgent; complete when capacity allows.

docs.title: Filtering Assignments
docs.text:
Use the Filters panel at the top of the tab to narrow the list:

  • Search — Filter by keyword in the assignment title.
  • Due Date — Filter by Overdue, This Week, Next Week, This Month, or No Due Date.
  • Survey — Show only assignments linked to a specific survey.
  • Element — Show only assignments linked to a specific element.
  • Status — Filter by a specific status.
  • Assignee — Filter by a specific team member.
  • Priority — Filter by priority level.

Click Clear all to reset all filters at once.

docs.title: Creating an Assignment
docs.text:
1. Click Create Assignment in the top-right corner of the Assignments tab.
  2. The create panel slides in from the right. Fill in the fields:
Field Required Description Title Yes A clear, descriptive title for the task. Description Yes The expected outcome or the specific next steps required. Assignee Yes The team member responsible for completing this work. Observers No Additional team members who receive status update notifications. Priority No High, Medium, or Low. Due Date No The target completion date. Survey No Link to a specific survey for context. Element No Link to a specific element — only available after a survey is selected.
  1. Click Create Assignment to save.

The new assignment appears in the table immediately.

docs.title: Editing an Assignment
docs.text: Click any assignment row to open the detail and edit panel. All fields can be updated. Click Save Changes when done.

docs.title: Marking Complete
docs.text: Click the circular checkbox on the left of an assignment row to toggle it complete. The status automatically moves to Done. Click the checkbox again to revert.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create the assignment and set the assignee, priority, and due date.
  2. Link it to the related survey or element so the assignee has context.
  3. The assignee updates the status as work moves forward (To Do → In Progress → Done).
  4. Use the comments and attachments inside the detail panel to keep communication on the record.
  5. A supervisor or account manager reviews completed work and confirms closure.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Site Detail
  • Tickets
  • Mobile Assignments and Tickets

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Attachments tab is the site file library. Use it to store and share reference documents, drawings, specifications, and any other files the team needs during or after site work. Files uploaded here are available to all users with access to the site.

Attachments tab for uploading and managing project files.

docs.title: Viewing Attachments
docs.text:
Use the toggle in the top-right corner to switch between two layouts:

  • List view — Files displayed in a table with name, size, and action controls. Best for scanning a long file list.
  • Grid view — Files displayed as visual cards with previews. Best for image or PDF files.

Your view preference is saved automatically and persists between sessions.

docs.title: Upload a Single File
docs.text:
1. Click Add Attachment in the top-right corner.
  2. Enter a file name for the attachment.
  3. Select your file using the file picker.
  4. Click Upload to confirm.

The file appears in the attachment list immediately.

docs.title: Upload Multiple Files at Once
docs.text:
1. Click the Upload Multiple option.
  2. Select one or more files from your device.
  3. A progress bar tracks each file individually during upload.
  4. All successfully uploaded files are added to the attachment list.

docs.title: Rename a File
docs.text:
1. Find the file in the list or grid.
  2. Open its row actions menu (three-dot icon).
  3. Select Rename, enter the new name, and confirm.

docs.title: Delete a File
docs.text:
1. Find the file in the list or grid.
  2. Open its row actions menu (three-dot icon).
  3. Select Delete and confirm.

Deleted files cannot be recovered.

docs.title: Best Practice
docs.text: Keep Attachments current. When files are updated — for example, a revised floor plan or a final spec sheet — upload the new version and remove or clearly rename the old one. Teams rely on this tab as a shared source of truth for project reference files.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Reports
  • Site Detail

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
A Site is the central workspace for work in OneSurvey. Surveys, media, assignments, tickets, and reports are all organized under a site.

Create a site from the Add Project modal.

docs.title: Available to
docs.text:
• Create site: Full seat users.
  • Manage site team: Manager, Owner.

If Add Project is not visible, your current access does not allow site creation.

docs.title: Create a Site
docs.text:
1. Open Sites.
  2. Select Add Project.
  3. Complete the form.
  4. Select Submit.

docs.title: Site Form Fields
docs.text: Field Required Description Name Yes Display name for the site. Project ID Yes Short identifier for the site. Spaces are converted to hyphens. Stage No Current lifecycle stage. Client Organization No Links the site to a client record.

docs.title: Stage Options
docs.text: Stage Description Lead in/Profile Initial profile and scoping. Pre-Design Survey and assessment phase. Take Off Quantity and scope takeoff. Design Active design work. Deployment Installation or deployment phase. LiveBuilt Final as-built documentation.

docs.title: Manage Site Team
docs.text:
Available to: Manager, Owner

  1. In the Project List, select the team avatar stack (or +) for a site.
  2. Search users by name or email.
  3. Add people to the invite list.
  4. Set project access level.
  5. Select Save.

docs.title: Project Access Levels
docs.text: Access Level Intended users What they can do Edit Full seat users Full survey and project actions. Restricted Edit Field seat users Field updates and photo workflows; limited design actions. View Viewer seat users View and comment only.

docs.title: Seat-Based Limits
docs.text:
• Full seat: Edit, Restricted Edit, or View.
  • Field seat: Restricted Edit or View.
  • Viewer seat: View only.
  • Owner and Manager organization roles are always locked to Edit.

docs.title: Manage Existing Sites
docs.text:
Available to: Manager, Owner (for menu actions)

From the row action menu in Project List: - Edit: update site details. - Copy Project: duplicate a site and its content. - Archive Site / Unarchive Site: move sites in or out of active views.

docs.title: Update Stage from List
docs.text:
Available to: Full seat users

Click the stage value in the Stage column to change stage directly.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
1. Add your first survey.
  2. Assign team members.
  3. Review site elements and data.
  4. Capture photos with Gallery or OneSnap.
  5. Track work with assignments and tickets.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Site Detail
  • Surveys Overview
  • Gallery
  • Assignments
  • Tickets
  • Clients

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Gallery is the site media workspace. All photos captured or uploaded for a site are organized here alongside albums for grouping, sharing, and reporting. Gallery has four views accessible from the controls at the top of the tab.

All Photos view with filtering and selection controls.

docs.title: Gallery (Default)
docs.text: The default landing view shows a visual overview of recent photos and albums for the site. Use this as a starting point before switching to All Photos or Albums for more focused work.

docs.title: All Photos
docs.text: Shows every photo uploaded to the site in a scrollable photo grid.

docs.title: Filtering Photos
docs.text:
Use the filter panel to narrow the photo list. Available filters:

Filter What it does Search Filter by photo name or metadata. Floor Plan Show only photos linked to a specific survey floor plan. Element Show only photos linked to a specific element. Category Filter by element category. User Show photos uploaded by a specific team member. Stage Filter by the project stage recorded when the photo was captured. Installation Status Filter by the installation status of the linked element at capture time. Date Range Show photos captured within a specific date window.

Applied filters can be cleared individually or all at once using Clear filters.

docs.title: Selecting Photos
docs.text:
Click Select Photo (top-right) to enter selection mode. In selection mode:

  • Click individual photo cards to select them.
  • Use Select All to select every photo currently visible.
  • Click the count button to deselect all.

With photos selected, click Create Album to group them into a new named album.

docs.title: All Albums
docs.text: Shows all albums created for the site as a grid of album cards.

docs.title: Creating an Album
docs.text:
Albums can be created two ways:

  • Select photos in All Photos view and click Create Album.
  • Use the Create Album button directly in the All Albums view and then add photos.

Give the album a name when prompted.

docs.title: Managing Albums
docs.text:
Each album card has an actions menu (three-dot icon):

  • Rename — Update the album name.
  • Delete — Remove the album. Photos inside are not deleted, only removed from the album.

Click an album card to open it in Album Detail view.

All Albums view for grouped photo packages.

docs.title: Album Detail
docs.text:
Opens a single album and shows all photos inside it. From here you can:

  • Scroll through the album's photos.
  • Add more photos to the album.
  • Remove individual photos from the album (without deleting the photo from the site).
  • Click any photo to open it in the full-screen photo viewer.

docs.title: Photo Viewer
docs.text:
Clicking any photo in Gallery opens a full-screen viewer. Inside the viewer you can:

  • Navigate between photos using the arrow controls.
  • Open the photo editor for annotations or basic adjustments.
  • Restore the original if edits were previously applied.

docs.title: Photo Context Menu
docs.text:
Right-click a photo (or open its three-dot menu) for quick actions:

  • Move or copy the photo to a different album.
  • Change the photo's stage tag.
  • Delete the photo from the site.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Capture or upload photos during or after a site visit (via the survey element panel, Gallery upload, or OneSnap).
  2. Use filters in All Photos to find photos by element, floor plan, date, or upload user.
  3. Select related photos and create albums to group them by scope or handoff package.
  4. Use albums when generating a Photo Report from the Reports tab.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Site Detail
  • Reports
  • Link Photos
  • OneSnap

docs.title: Moved
docs.text: This page moved to Gallery.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Site Detail is the main workspace for a site. When you open a site from the Project List, you land here by default on the Surveys tab. All of the site's work — surveys, elements, data, files, photos, assignments, and tickets — is organized across tabs at the top of the page.

The page header shows the site name. Users with the appropriate role can rename the site directly from the header.

Site Detail workspace with surveys, media, and operations tabs.

docs.title: Tabs
docs.text:
Site Detail contains the following tabs. Tab visibility depends on your role and seat:

Tab What it contains Surveys All surveys for this site. Create new surveys, open existing ones, and manage floor plans. Site Elements The element library for this site. Browse, search, and favorite elements before placing them in surveys. Site Data Tabular views of element, survey, installation, and bill of materials data across the whole site. Attachments Documents, drawings, and reference files uploaded to the site. Reports Generated report outputs ready for download or sharing. Gallery All site photos and albums. Upload, filter, organize, and build photo packages. Tickets Service issues and support tickets linked to this site. Assignments Tracked work items owned by team members.

Tab visibility

Users with a Viewer seat cannot access the Assignments tab. All other tabs are visible to all users with site access.

docs.title: Navigating Between Tabs
docs.text:
Click any tab name to switch views. The URL updates to include a ?tab= parameter so refreshing the page returns you to the same tab.

Deep links can also open a specific record directly:

  • A URL with ?tab=assignments&assignment=<id> opens the Assignments tab with that assignment's detail panel open.
  • A URL with ?tab=tickets&ticket=<id> opens the Tickets tab with that ticket's detail panel open.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
The tabs support a natural project progression:

  1. Surveys — Build floor plans, place elements, and keep element details current.
  2. Gallery — Upload and organize site photos. Link photos to elements from within the survey or directly from Gallery.
  3. Site Data — Review element and survey data in bulk before generating reports or handing off.
  4. Assignments — Track execution work and team ownership during active phases.
  5. Tickets — Log and resolve service issues or follow-up items.
  6. Reports — Generate and download final deliverables.
  7. Attachments — Store reference drawings, specs, and deliverable files.

docs.title: Access Notes
docs.text:
• Archived sites open in read-only mode. Content can be viewed but not edited.
  • If you open a site that belongs to a different organization than your current one, the app will automatically switch organizations if you are a member of both.
  • Users who are not members of a site's organization will see an access denied message.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Create a Site
  • Surveys Overview
  • Site Elements
  • Site Data
  • Attachments
  • Reports
  • Gallery
  • Assignments
  • Tickets

docs.title: Moved
docs.text: This page moved to Gallery.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Reports tab is where generated report outputs are collected, downloaded, and managed. Reports are initiated from this tab using a step-by-step wizard, processed server-side, and then appear in the list below the wizard once complete.

docs.title: Generating a Report
docs.text: Click the button to start a new report. The wizard walks through three steps:

docs.title: Step 1 — Select a Report Type
docs.text:
Choose from the available report types:

Report Type What it outputs Bill of Materials Element quantities grouped by type across the site, suitable for procurement or scope review. Element Information Custom field data (model number, serial number, IP address, etc.) for each placed element. Survey Data Survey-level data for each placed element — status, survey fields, and element details. Installation Data Installation details for each element — access notes, technician, timing, and installation status. Floor Plan A PDF of the survey floor plan with elements rendered on the image. Photo Report A formatted PDF of selected site photos, organized for sharing or client handoff.

Click the card for the report type you want and proceed to the next step.

docs.title: Step 2 — Configure the Report
docs.text:
Configuration options vary by report type. Common options include:

  • Surveys to include — Select one survey or all surveys on the site.
  • Elements to include — Filter by element type or include all.
  • Fields to include — Choose which data fields appear in the output.
  • Paper size — Select the output size (e.g., Letter, A4).
  • Report name — Give the report a custom title that will appear in the Reports list.
  • Cover page — Some report types support a branded cover page.

docs.title: Step 3 — Generate
docs.text:
Click Generate. The report is queued for server-side processing. A confirmation message confirms it has been queued successfully. Once processing is complete, the report appears in the Reports list on this tab.

Processing time

Reports are generated server-side and may take a moment depending on the size of the site. If your report does not appear immediately, wait a moment and refresh the Reports tab.

docs.title: Viewing and Downloading Reports
docs.text:
All generated reports appear in the list with a title, report type, and creation date.

  • Click the report title or Download button to download the file.
  • Reports are delivered as PDF or XLSX (spreadsheet) format depending on the type selected.

docs.title: Deleting Reports
docs.text:
To remove an outdated or incorrect report:

  1. Find the report in the list.
  2. Click the Delete action (trash icon or row actions menu).
  3. Confirm the deletion.

Deleted reports cannot be recovered. Use the wizard to regenerate if needed.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Site Data
  • Gallery
  • Attachments

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Site Data provides tabular views of element and survey information collected across the entire site. Use it to review, validate, and check data in bulk before generating reports or handing off a project. It is the best way to confirm completeness across all surveys at once.

docs.title: Tabs
docs.text: Site Data has four tabs, toggled at the top of the page:

docs.title: Survey Information
docs.text:
A table of all elements placed across every survey on the site. Each row represents a placed element with its associated survey-level data — element name, type, label, status, survey name, and related fields.

Use this tab to:

  • Confirm all elements have been documented correctly.
  • Search and filter by element type, survey, or status.
  • Validate coverage before running a Survey Data report.

docs.title: Installation Information
docs.text:
Installation-specific details captured for each element, such as installation status, access restrictions, technician notes, and timing information.

Use this tab to:

  • Verify installation data is complete before a deployment phase or handoff.
  • Filter by installation status to identify incomplete or in-progress items.

docs.title: Element Information
docs.text:
The custom data fields recorded for each element — such as model number, serial number, IP address, asset tag, or any other fields configured in your element library.

Use this tab to:

  • Confirm key element data fields are populated before generating an Element Information report.
  • Search for specific elements or field values across the full site.

docs.title: Bill of Materials
docs.text:
A materials summary based on the elements placed across the site, grouped by element type and quantity.

Use this tab to:

  • Review totals by element type before generating a Bill of Materials report.
  • Validate scope against the original project estimate.

Bill of Materials tab with grouped quantities by element type.

docs.title: Filters and Search
docs.text: Each tab includes a search field and filter options. Use the rows per page selector and pagination controls at the bottom of each table to navigate large datasets.

docs.title: Relationship to Reports
docs.text: Site Data is a pre-report validation step. Once the data looks correct here, go to the Reports tab to generate and download the corresponding outputs.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Reports
  • Element Information
  • Element Sidebar

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The Site Elements tab is the element library for a site. It shows all element types available to place on surveys, organized by category. Before starting survey work, use this tab to browse available elements and set up favorites for faster access during survey editing.

Browse and search element libraries before placement.

docs.title: Element Libraries
docs.text:
The tab has two libraries, toggled at the top:

Library What it contains Universal Elements Standard elements available to all sites in the organization. Managed by your organization's admins. My Library Custom elements specific to your organization, organized into super categories and categories.

Both libraries organize elements in a hierarchy: Super Category → Category → Elements. Click a category header to expand or collapse it and reveal the elements inside.

docs.title: Searching Elements
docs.text: Use the Search element field in the top-right corner to search across both libraries at once. Results update as you type and show matches from Universal Elements and My Library together.

docs.title: Favorites
docs.text: The Favorites dropdown (top-right, shown as Favorites X/20) lets you bookmark elements for quick access on this site. You can save up to 20 favorites per site.

docs.title: Adding a Favorite
docs.text:
1. Find an element in either library tab.
  2. Click the star icon on the element tile.
  3. The element is added to your Favorites list.

docs.title: Removing a Favorite
docs.text:
Click the star icon again on any favorited element to remove it.

The Favorites dropdown shows your current saved count (e.g., Favorites 4/20). Your favorites appear in the survey's element panel sidebar for fast drag-and-drop placement.

docs.title: Using Elements in Surveys
docs.text:
Elements are placed onto floor plans by dragging from the element library panel inside the survey workspace. The library in that panel mirrors the same Universal Elements and My Library shown here. Favorites appear at the top for quick access.

For step-by-step instructions on placing elements, see Drag and Drop Elements.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Drag and Drop Elements
  • Canvas Workspace Basics
  • Element Information
  • Site Data

docs.title: Moved
docs.text: This page moved to Site Detail.

docs.title: Moved
docs.text: This page moved to Assignments.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Tickets track service issues, support requests, and reactive follow-up work tied to a specific site. Unlike Assignments — which track planned, owned execution tasks — Tickets are used for problems discovered on site, vendor coordination, repair requests, and anything that needs to be logged, tracked, and resolved.

Each ticket can be linked to a specific survey and element so the issue stays in context.

docs.title: The Tickets Table
docs.text:
The Tickets tab shows all tickets for the site in a table:

Column Description (Checkbox) Click to mark the ticket complete or closed. Title Ticket title with its auto-assigned ticket number (e.g., #42). Floor Plan The linked survey name, if set. Element The linked element and its position number, if set. Status Current status — click to change directly from the table. Priority Current priority — click to change directly from the table. Category The ticket category badge. SLA SLA timer badge, if the client has SLA settings configured. Created Date the ticket was created.

Click any row to open the ticket detail panel.

docs.title: Statuses
docs.text: Status When to use it Open Issue logged, not yet addressed. Pending Vendor Waiting on an external vendor before work can continue. In Progress Actively being worked on. Waiting on Parts Work is paused until materials or components arrive. Scheduled Work is confirmed for a specific date. Completed Issue has been resolved. Closed Ticket is fully done and closed out.

docs.title: Priorities
docs.text: Priority When to use it Critical Requires immediate attention. High Urgent but not immediately blocking. Medium Standard priority. Low Non-urgent; resolve when capacity allows.

docs.title: Categories
docs.text: Category Use for RMA Return merchandise authorization requests. Maintenance Scheduled or reactive maintenance work. Programming Software, firmware, or configuration issues. Cabling Wiring or cabling problems. Vendor Work Work coordinated with or awaiting an external vendor. Other Issues that do not fit the above categories.

docs.title: Filtering Tickets
docs.text:
Use the Filters panel at the top of the Tickets tab:

  • Search — Filter by title, description, or ticket number.
  • Status — Show tickets with a specific status.
  • Priority — Filter by priority level.
  • Category — Filter by category type.
  • My Tickets toggle — Show only tickets assigned to or created by you.

Click Clear all to reset all active filters.

docs.title: Creating a Ticket
docs.text:
1. Click Create Ticket in the top-right corner of the Tickets tab.
  2. The create panel opens on the right. Fill in the fields:
Field Required Description Title Yes A clear description of the issue. Description No Additional context — what the problem is and what resolution is expected. Status Yes Set the initial status (typically Open). Priority Yes Critical, High, Medium, or Low. Category Yes The type of issue. Survey No Link to the survey where the issue was found. Element No Link to the specific element involved. Requires a survey to be selected first.
  1. Click Submit to create the ticket. A ticket number is assigned automatically.

docs.title: Ticket Detail Panel
docs.text:
Clicking a ticket row opens the detail panel on the right side:

  • Title and ticket number — Edit directly from the panel.
  • Status, Priority, Category — Change any of these from the panel.
  • Description — View or update the issue description.
  • Linked Assignments — Any assignments converted from or linked to this ticket appear here with their current status.
  • Floor Plan — A clickable link to the linked survey, if set.
  • Element — The linked element with a button to navigate directly to it on the survey canvas.
  • Creator — Who created the ticket and when.
  • SLA — SLA timer and status summary, if configured for the client.
  • Attachments — Files uploaded directly to this ticket.
  • Comments — A threaded comment section for team communication about this issue.
  • Audit Trail — A full history of every change made to the ticket — who changed what and when.

docs.title: Updating Status or Priority from the Table
docs.text: You do not need to open the detail panel to change a ticket's status or priority. The Status and Priority columns in the table are interactive dropdowns. Click the current value to open the dropdown and select a new one directly from the table row.

docs.title: Converting a Ticket to an Assignment
docs.text:
When a ticket becomes planned execution work that needs to be owned and tracked, convert it:

  1. Open the ticket detail panel.
  2. Use the Convert to Assignment action.
  3. An assignment is created from the ticket's details. The ticket and assignment remain linked.

docs.title: Typical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create the ticket and set status, priority, and category.
  2. Link the related survey and element so the issue has location context.
  3. Add photos or files as attachments to document the issue visually.
  4. Collaborate via the comment thread — keep the team informed as the issue progresses.
  5. Update the status as work moves forward (Open → In Progress → Completed → Closed).
  6. If the ticket turns into planned work, convert it to an Assignment.

docs.title: Access and Roles
docs.text: Use this page to understand what users can do based on organization role and seat type.

docs.title: Organization Roles
docs.text: Role What it controls Owner Full organization control, including assigning Owner access. Manager Manages users, operations, and organization settings. Member Works in sites based on seat type and assigned project access.

docs.title: Seat Types
docs.text: Seat Typical capability Full Full planning and editing workflows. Field Field execution workflows and updates. Viewer View and comment access with limited editing.

docs.title: Quick Rules
docs.text:
• Owner and Manager roles require a Full seat.
  • Visible menus depend on both role and seat.
  • Project-level access (Edit, Restricted Edit, View) is also limited by seat type.

docs.title: Feature Access Snapshot
docs.text: Area Who typically has access Sites and Surveys Most users with project access Assignments Field and Full seat users Tickets Viewer, Field, and Full seat users Users / Clients Managers and Owners Audit Logs Owners

docs.text: If a button or menu is missing, ask your manager or owner to review your role and seat assignment.

docs.title: Related Pages

docs.title: FAQ
docs.text: Use this FAQ for quick answers to common OneSurvey workflow questions. For full steps, use the linked feature pages.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• Access and Login
  • Sites and Floor Plans
  • Survey Canvas and Documentation
  • Assignments, Tickets, and Work Tracking
  • Site Data and Reports
  • Mobile and Field Use
  • Need More Help

docs.title: Access and Login
docs.text:
How do I get access to OneSurvey?

OneSurvey access is invitation-based. A manager or owner adds you from the Users page and sends your invite email. After you accept the invite and complete first-time setup, you can sign in and start working in your assigned sites.

Read more.

I did not receive my invite, or my invite link expired. What should I do?

First check spam or junk folders. If the email is not there, or the link is expired, ask your manager or owner to resend the invitation from Users. If a link was revoked, a new invite is required.

Read more.

How do I reset my password?

From the login screen, select Forgot Password and submit your email. Use the reset link from the email to set a new password, then sign in again. If the email does not arrive, ask your manager or owner to confirm your account status.

Read more.

Why do I see different menus than my teammate?

Menu visibility depends on role, seat type, and project-level access. Two users can be on the same site and still see different options if permissions differ. This is expected and keeps actions aligned to each user’s responsibilities.

Read more.

docs.title: Sites and Floor Plans
docs.text:
How do I create a new site?

Go to Sites and use Add Project to create the site workspace. Add core details first, then assign team members and start floor plan setup. If the create action is missing, your current access may not include site creation.

Read more.

What floor plan file types can I upload?

You can upload common image formats and PDF files for floor plans. In most workflows, teams use PNG/JPG for single plans and PDF for multi-page plan sets. Keep files clear and readable so element placement and field documentation stay accurate.

Read more.

Can I upload a multi-page PDF and create multiple surveys?

Yes. During upload, select the pages you want to include and OneSurvey creates a survey for each selected page. This is useful when one site has multiple floors, wings, or system layouts in one PDF.

Read more.

Why do I need to set scale before uploading?

Scale connects plan pixels to real-world distance, which keeps measurements meaningful across your workflow. It affects distance-based review like path lengths and coverage tuning. Setting scale upfront avoids rework later in camera placement and field validation.

Read more.

Can I update the floor plan or re-scale later?

Yes. You can replace the survey background and update scale when a revised drawing is issued. After changes, recheck key placements so element context and distance-based views remain accurate.

Read more.

docs.title: Survey Canvas and Documentation
docs.text:
What is the fastest way to place many elements?

Use Favorites plus drag-and-drop from the element library. Place elements in batches by area, then fill in details from the Element Sidebar. This works well for camera and device layouts across large floor plans.

Read more.

What is the difference between Universal Elements and My Library?

Universal Elements are shared standard element types available across your organization. My Library contains organization-specific element types used for your own standards or customer needs. Most teams use both together in the same survey workflow.

Read more.

When should I use Drawings and Walls instead of placed elements?

Use placed elements for trackable assets (like cameras or devices) that need status, assignments, tickets, and data fields. Use Drawings and Walls for visual context, structural markup, and planning notes. In practice, teams combine both for clear design intent and handoff.

Read more.

Where do I update element details like FoV, status, assignments, and tickets?

Select an element on the canvas to open the Element Sidebar. Use its menus to update coverage, field details, work tracking, and related actions in one place. This keeps element-level context tied to the exact location on the floor plan.

Read more.

What is the best way to attach photos and comments to the right element?

Select the element first, then add photos and comments directly from that element’s workflow. This ensures evidence is tied to the correct asset and location. Use Gallery later for site-level review and sharing.

Read more.

docs.title: Assignments, Tickets, and Work Tracking
docs.text:
What is the difference between Assignments and Tickets?

Assignments are planned, owned tasks for execution work. Tickets are issue tracking records for problems, service requests, and follow-up. A common pattern is to capture a field issue as a ticket, then convert to an assignment when work ownership is defined.

Read more.

Can I convert a ticket into an assignment?

Yes. Open the ticket detail panel and use Convert to Assignment. The resulting assignment stays linked so your team can track issue context and execution progress together.

Read more.

Where do I see work assigned to me?

Use My Assignments for your task queue across sites. In site context, also use the Assignments tab for local planning and status checks. If you work issues, use ticket filters to focus on your own tickets.

Read more.

docs.title: Site Data and Reports
docs.text:
When should I use Site Data vs Reports?

Use Site Data for live validation, filtering, and cleanup before handoff. Use Reports when you are ready to generate deliverables for stakeholders. A reliable workflow is: validate in Site Data first, then generate and export.

Read more.

Which report type should I choose?

Choose by outcome: Bill of Materials for quantity totals, Element Information for asset fields, and Floor Plan or Photo Report for visual deliverables. Teams often generate more than one report type for design review and final handoff. Pick the output your audience needs first, then add supporting reports.

Read more.

Why is my report not showing up yet?

Reports are queued and generated server-side, so larger sites can take longer. Wait briefly, refresh the Reports tab, and confirm you are in the correct site and report view. If it still does not appear, regenerate and confirm your selected configuration.

Read more.

docs.title: Mobile and Field Use
docs.text:
What can I do on mobile vs desktop?

Desktop is best for planning-heavy work and broad site administration. Mobile is optimized for in-field updates like survey review, photos, assignments, and tickets. Teams commonly plan on desktop, then capture and update from phone or tablet on site.

Read more.

Can I capture field updates on mobile and finish review on desktop?

Yes. OneSurvey supports cross-device workflows so mobile capture and desktop review stay in one connected record. This is a standard handoff pattern for field teams documenting progress during installation and review teams finalizing quality checks at a workstation.

Read more.

docs.title: Need More Help
docs.text:
• Contact support: https://www.onesurveyapp.com/contact
  • Ask your manager or owner to confirm role and seat access if a feature is missing.

docs.title: Glossary
docs.text:
Assignment: A tracked work item with owner, priority, status, comments, and history.

Client: A customer record used to group sites, OneSnap activity, and SLA settings.

Gallery: The site media area where photos and albums are managed together.

Invitation: Admin-sent access email used to join an organization and complete account setup.

OneSnap: A fast photo workflow for capture, albums, map review, and reporting.

Owner: The highest organization role with full administrative authority.

Role: Organization authority level: Owner, Manager, or Member.

Seat: Capability level in the app: Full, Field, or Viewer.

Ticket: A service or issue tracking item tied to site context.

Version History: Saved survey states that let teams review and restore earlier versions.

docs.title: Release Notes
docs.text: Use this page to summarize important product updates for end users.

docs.title: Latest Highlights
docs.text:
• Site Gallery manages photos and albums in one place.
  • Assignments and Tickets are active in Site Detail and mobile.
  • OneSnap includes Gallery, Albums, Map, Reports, and Archive.

docs.title: Suggested Format for Future Updates
docs.text:
1. Date.
  2. What changed.
  3. Who is affected.
  4. Any action required.

docs.title: Surveys Overview
docs.text:
Surveys are where teams create floor plan layouts, place elements, and maintain field-ready site information.

Survey list and status overview for a site.

docs.title: Most Used
docs.text: Upload a Floor Plan Drag and Drop Elements Element Sidebar Element Information Link Photos

docs.title: Canvas Workspace Basics
docs.text:
The canvas is the primary survey workspace for placing elements, adding context, and reviewing progress before handoff.

Use the canvas to place elements and update details in context.

docs.title: Core Actions
docs.text:
• Pan and zoom to the area you are documenting.
  • Select, move, and update placed elements.
  • Open the Element Sidebar to manage details, photos, and tickets.
  • Switch floor plans or versions as needed.

Set floor-plan scale before detailed placement for accurate mapping.

docs.title: Practical Workflow
docs.text:
1. Open the target floor plan.
  2. Place or select elements on the canvas.
  3. Update details in the Element Sidebar.
  4. Use Visibility Filters to validate the final view.

docs.title: Guides by Topic
docs.text:
Survey Setup

Set up the canvas and floor plan before detailed field work.

  • Upload a Floor Plan
  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Drawings and Walls
Element and Media Work

Place elements and capture the visual context needed for delivery.

  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Element Information
  • Link Photos
Quality Control

Review standards and history before sharing results with your team.

  • Visibility Filters
  • Element Sidebar
  • Version History

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Sites Overview
  • Mobile Surveys
  • Reports
  • FAQ

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Use Paths to show cable runs, route direction, or connection guidance for an element on the floor plan.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Path workflow from creating a line to extending and adjusting sections.

docs.title: Open Path Tools
docs.text:
1. Open a survey and select an element on the canvas.
  2. In the Element Sidebar, open Path.
  3. Choose a line color before you start.

If you do not see the Path option, your current mode or seat may not include path editing.

docs.title: Create and Extend a Path
docs.text:
1. Click Add Line to create the first path section for the selected element.
  2. Click Add Section to extend the route one segment at a time.
  3. Repeat until the route matches your intended run.

Each added section continues from the end of the previous section, so the route stays connected.

docs.title: Edit Path Shape on the Canvas
docs.text:
• Click a path to select it.
  • Drag a start or end marker to reposition that point.
  • Neighboring sections update with the move so the route remains continuous.
  • Length values update automatically as you move points.

docs.title: Remove Sections or Delete a Line
docs.text:
• Click Delete Section to remove the last section from the selected line.
  • If a line has only one section left, deleting that section removes the full line.

docs.title: Review Length and Visibility
docs.text:
• The sidebar shows:
  • selected line length
  • total length across lines on the selected element
  • unit in Feet
  • Use Visibility Filters to toggle Show Path on or off during review.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Survey Overview
  • Element Sidebar
  • Element Information
  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Drawings and Walls

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Comments keep site conversation connected to the exact element or survey context.

Comments panel tied to survey and element context.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Select the element.
  2. Open comments.
  3. Add your note.
  4. Save so the team can review.

Comments tab for reviewing thread history.

Use comment actions for follow-up and cleanup.

docs.title: Best Practice
docs.text:
Keep comments short, specific, and action-oriented.

Preview comments quickly from summary cards.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Element Information
  • Assignments
  • Tickets

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The Element Sidebar opens when you select an element on the survey canvas. It is the main place to update element details, field documentation, and workflow actions before you move to the next location.

docs.title: Sidebar Menus
docs.text:
• Photos: attach and review visual proof. See Link Photos.
  • Comments: add context and field notes. See Commenting.
  • FoV: adjust field-of-view direction, depth, and coverage settings. See FoV Adjustment.
  • Assignments: route work to team members. See Assignments.
  • Tickets: track issues and follow-up actions. See Tickets.
  • Installation: capture install status and details used in reporting. See Element Information and Site Data.

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Select the element on the canvas.
  2. Use the sidebar menus to update details and attach evidence.
  3. Confirm the element looks correct in context.
  4. Save and continue to the next element.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Element Information
  • FoV Adjustment
  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Version History

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Use drag and drop to place elements from the library onto the survey canvas. This is the fastest way to build out camera, device, and field-condition coverage across a floor plan.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Drag and drop workflow from library to placement on the canvas.

docs.title: Open the Element Library
docs.text:
• Use Favorites for your most-used items.
  • Use Universal Elements for shared standard elements.
  • Use My Library for your organization-specific elements.

Favorites are best for repeat workflows and support up to 20 saved elements.

docs.title: Place Elements on the Canvas
docs.text:
1. Open a survey.
  2. From the left library, select an element.
  3. Drag the element onto the exact location on the floor plan.
  4. Drop to place it.
  5. Confirm the success message, then continue placing or edit details.

When placement succeeds, you will see an Element added to floorplan confirmation.

Drag elements from the library directly onto the canvas.

docs.title: What Happens After Drop
docs.text:
• The placed element is automatically selected so you can update details right away.
  • A default FoV and standard element options are prepared for the new element.
  • If you place the same element type multiple times, OneSurvey keeps placement order clear for review.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Place elements at real-world reference points for cleaner reporting and handoff.
  • Update key details in Element Information after placement.
  • Use FoV Adjustment to verify direction, angle, and depth before final review.
  • Add evidence quickly with Link Photos and context with Commenting.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Survey Overview
  • Element Sidebar
  • Element Information
  • FoV Adjustment
  • Link Photos

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Draw mode helps you add structure and context directly on the floor plan. Use it for walls, lines, arrows, shapes, and text notes so teams can review scope and field conditions clearly.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Draw mode walkthrough for creating and adjusting walls and other annotations.

docs.title: Draw Tools Available
docs.text:
• Walls: create multi-point wall runs and room boundaries.
  • Line: add straight reference lines.
  • Arrow: show direction or flow.
  • Ellipse / Rectangle: call out areas and zones.
  • Freeform: sketch irregular boundaries or notes.
  • Text: place editable text labels on the canvas.

Shape tools in draw mode for quick visual markup and annotation.

docs.title: Draw and Edit Walls
docs.text:
1. Enter draw mode and choose Walls.
  2. Click or tap to place points.
  3. Continue placing points to create multi-segment walls.
  4. Select the wall to edit its details in the drawing sidebar.

If you are actively drawing, use Finish Drawing when you want to stop adding points.

docs.title: Wall Sections and Properties
docs.text:
When a wall is selected, you can edit all sections together or a single section.

  • Section type: choose from Concrete/Masonry, Metal, Drywall/Wood, Glass, or Other/Unknown.
  • Section name: add a label for easier review.
  • Color, thickness, opacity: control visual style for clarity.
  • Length display: view total wall length or section length.
  • Add Point: extend or reshape a wall.
  • Delete Section: remove one section (when a wall has multiple sections).
  • Delete Wall: remove the wall entirely.

docs.title: Work with Shapes and Text
docs.text:
• Use Line and Arrow for direction and quick callouts.
  • Use Ellipse or Rectangle with Outline or Filled style for area highlights.
  • Use Freeform for irregular shapes that do not fit standard geometry.
  • Use Text to place labels and notes; then edit text content, font size, color, and opacity.

docs.title: Best-Practice Workflow
docs.text:
1. Add key walls first.
  2. Add shapes and text labels to clarify intent.
  3. Adjust visual style (color/thickness/opacity) so markups stay readable.
  4. Recheck visibility before handoff.

Walls are used in coverage calculations, while other shapes are visual annotations.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Canvas Workspace Basics
  • FoV Adjustment
  • Visibility Filters
  • Adding a Path

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Element Information is where you review and update details for each placed element.

docs.title: Typical Fields
docs.text:
• Name or label.
  • Status.
  • Installation details.
  • Survey-specific details.
  • Linked comments and photos.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Select an element.
  2. Open the element panel.
  3. Update required fields.
  4. Save changes.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• FoV Adjustment
  • Link Photos
  • Commenting
  • Element Sidebar

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Use FoV (Field of View) Adjustment to control how each camera or directional element covers the floor plan. This is where you tune coverage direction, width, depth, and visibility styling so teams can review intent clearly.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

FoV panel walkthrough for direction, angle, depth, and multi-FoV configuration.

docs.title: Open FoV Controls
docs.text:
1. Open a survey and select an element on the canvas.
  2. In the Element Sidebar, select FoV.
  3. Use the Field of View panel to update coverage settings.

docs.title: What You Can Adjust
docs.text:
• Direction: rotate where the FoV points.
  • Angle: widen or narrow the coverage cone.
  • Depth: increase or reduce how far coverage extends.
  • Color: set the FoV color (HEX) for clarity on busy plans.
  • Opacity: control how transparent the coverage overlay appears.

docs.title: Multiple FoVs on One Element
docs.text:
One element can use multiple FoVs when you need separate viewing zones.

  1. Open FoV controls for the selected element.
  2. Use Add FoV to create another coverage zone.
  3. Select each FoV in the list and adjust its direction, angle, depth, and color independently.
  4. Remove extra FoVs you no longer need.

The current UI supports adding multiple FoVs (up to four per element). One FoV must remain.

FoV menu with multiple FoV entries and quick Add FoV controls.

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Place the element on the survey canvas.
  2. Set direction first, then tune angle and depth.
  3. Adjust color/opacity so coverage remains readable with other elements.
  4. Recheck placement with Visibility Filters before final review.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Element Sidebar

  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Visibility Filters

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Link photos to elements to keep visual documentation tied to the exact location.

Upload photos directly from the linked element workflow.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Select an element.
  2. Open element photos.
  3. Upload one or more images.
  4. Confirm the images are linked to the correct element.

docs.title: Common Actions
docs.text:
• Preview photos.
  • Annotate photos.
  • Move a photo to another element.
  • Remove photos.

Photo menu options for preview, edits, and re-linking.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Gallery
  • Commenting

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Upload a floor plan to create the survey canvas your team will use for element placement, documentation, and tracking.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

End-to-end floor plan upload workflow from file selection through scaling and publish.

docs.title: Start the Upload
docs.text:
1. Open your site and go to the Surveys tab.
  2. Select Add Floor Plan.
  3. Choose a supported file:
  4. JPEG/JPG
  5. PNG
  6. GIF
  7. WebP
  8. SVG
  9. PDF (single page or multi-page)

If you upload a multi-page PDF, select which pages to include. Each selected page becomes its own survey/floor plan.

Start from the Surveys tab using Add Floor Plan.

docs.title: Name and Scale Before Upload
docs.text:
1. Enter a survey name for each selected page.
  2. Select Set Scale (or Update Scale).
  3. In the scale screen, double-click to draw a reference line on the plan.
  4. Enter the real-world length (Feet).
  5. Continue, then select Upload.

Scale is required and is used for measurement-based features across the survey experience.

Edit Details view where you name surveys and confirm scale before uploading.

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Keep floor plan files clean and high-contrast for easier review.
  • Use clear names for each floor or area.
  • Verify scale first so distance-based results stay accurate.
  • If your layout changes, update the floor plan and re-check scale before continuing work.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text:
• Surveys Overview
  • Drag and Drop Elements
  • Element Sidebar

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Version History lets you review and restore previous survey states.

Review snapshots and restore previous survey versions.

docs.title: Steps
docs.text:
1. Open Version History.
  2. Review saved versions.
  3. Restore a version when needed.

docs.title: Why It Helps
docs.text: Version history provides a clear change trail and a safe rollback option.

docs.title: Related Pages
docs.text: • Canvas Workspace Basics

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Visibility Filters help you focus on the elements that matter for the task in front of you. Use them to quickly switch between different system layouts and reduce visual noise on dense floor plans.

Your browser does not support the video tag.

Switch between filtered system views to review the same survey from different layout perspectives.

docs.title: What You Can Filter
docs.text:
• Hide Element: hide specific element types from the canvas using the element tiles in the filter panel.
  • Search: find elements by element name, element label, or category.
  • Filter Category: include only selected categories; this supports both shared and organization-specific libraries.
  • Installation Status: filter by status values:
  • Installed
  • Proposed
  • Pending Replacement
  • Pending Upgrade
  • Pending Removal
  • N/A

docs.title: Display Toggles
docs.text:
• Show Number: show or hide each element's number badge.
  • Show Notifications: show or hide badges for photos, comments, assignments, and open tickets.
  • Show Path: show or hide route/path overlays on the canvas.

These are display controls only and do not delete or change your saved survey data.

docs.title: Filter Panel Reference
docs.text: Visibility Filter panel with element, category, status, and display controls.

docs.title: Recommended Workflow
docs.text:
1. Open Visibility Filter from the survey canvas.
  2. Hide unrelated element types first.
  3. Narrow by category and installation status.
  4. Toggle numbers, notifications, or paths based on what you are reviewing.
  5. Use Reset Filter to return to the full default view.
